assertion_results.text: Migration uses CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY (not a regular CREATE INDEX)
Migration includes an estimate or discussion of how long the index creation will take on 100M rows
assertion_results.evidence: Header states 'Estimated duration: 30-60 minutes on 100M rows'

assertion_results.text: Impact analysis discusses the effect on write performance (insert overhead from maintaining the new index)
assertion_results.evidence: Header states '~5-15% insert latency overhead after index built'

assertion_results.text: A rollback is provided that drops the index (DROP INDEX or DROP INDEX CONCURRENTLY)
assertion_results.evidence: rollback.sql: 'DROP INDEX CONCURRENTLY IF EXISTS idx_events_customer_id_created_at'

assertion_results.text: Verification queries confirm the index exists and is valid (not in an invalid state from a failed CONCURRENTLY build)
The migration notes that CONCURRENTLY cannot run inside a transaction block
assertion_results.evidence: Migration comment: 'CONCURRENTLY cannot run inside a transaction block'

assertion_results.text: A deployment runbook recommends running during off-peak hours or low-traffic periods
assertion_results.evidence: Pre-flight includes 'Schedule during off-peak hours' with a query to identify lowest-traffic hours from the last 7 days

assertion_results.text: Lock impact analysis mentions that CONCURRENTLY avoids blocking writes (SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE vs SHARE lock)
assertion_results.evidence: Header states 'SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE (non-blocking, allows concurrent reads/writes)'
